short_definition: Identifies the version of the schema specification that this Digital Product Passport instance conforms to.
Indicates the current publication or lifecycle status of the Digital Product Passport record.
Describes the level of detail at which passport data is reported, such as model-level or individual unit-level granularity.
The date and time at which the most recent change to this Digital Product Passport was recorded.
A globally unique, persistent URI that unambiguously identifies this specific Digital Product Passport instance.
A globally unique URI that distinctly identifies the physical battery unit or model described by this passport.
A manufacturer-assigned identifier that designates a particular battery model or product line.
A unique alphanumeric code assigned to an individual battery unit for traceability purposes.
A unique identifier assigned to the economic operator responsible for placing the battery on the market.
A unique identifier assigned to the legal entity that manufactured the battery.
A unique identifier for the manufacturing facility or plant where the battery was produced.
Contact and descriptive details about the economic operator responsible for the battery's market placement.
Name, address, and contact details for the company that manufactured the battery.
The geographic location, including country and optionally city or region, where the battery was manufactured.
The year and month in which the battery was manufactured, expressed in YYYY-MM format.

attribute_name: Date of Putting Battery into Service
short_definition: The year and month the battery was first placed into operational service or commissioned.
The duration covered by the manufacturer's warranty for the battery, expressed as an end date in YYYY-MM format.
The regulatory classification of the battery such as portable, light means of transport, industrial, or electric vehicle.
The total mass of the complete battery pack or system as supplied, measured in kilograms.
The current lifecycle stage of the battery, such as original use, repurposed, remanufactured, or end-of-life.
A URI referencing the mandatory symbol indicating that the battery must be collected separately from general household waste.
Chemical hazard symbols or textual indicators that must appear on batteries containing cadmium or lead above threshold concentrations.
The label and corresponding performance class letter assigned to the battery based on its lifecycle carbon footprint intensity.
The recommended fire suppression agent or method to be used in the event of a battery fire.
Explanatory text describing what each label, marking, or symbol printed on the battery or its packaging signifies.
A URI linking to the official EU Declaration of Conformity document certifying compliance with applicable regulations.
A URI pointing to the results or summary of test reports carried out to demonstrate regulatory compliance.
Total lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions attributable to the battery, expressed per kilowatt-hour of rated energy.
The share of the battery's total carbon footprint attributable to the extraction and preprocessing of raw materials.
The portion of the battery's carbon footprint arising from the main manufacturing and assembly processes.
The carbon emissions associated with transporting and distributing the battery from manufacturing site to market.
The carbon footprint contribution from end-of-life treatment and recycling of the battery and its components.
A letter grade or class designation ranking the battery's carbon intensity relative to regulatory thresholds.
A publicly accessible URI linking to the full lifecycle carbon footprint study underpinning the reported values.
The total lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions of the battery expressed as an absolute value in kilograms of CO2 equivalent.
A URI linking to the published supply chain due diligence report covering responsible sourcing of battery materials.
Declarations or certifications from independent third-party organisations confirming adherence to recognised responsible sourcing schemes.
Quantitative or categorical indicators measuring the social and environmental performance of the battery supply chain.
A description of the electrochemical technology used in the battery, such as lithium-ion, nickel-metal hydride, or lead-acid.
A list of materials classified as critical raw materials that are present in the battery above defined threshold concentrations.
Lists of the active materials and chemical compounds used in the cathode, anode, and electrolyte of the battery.
Identification of hazardous chemical substances present in the battery that require disclosure under applicable regulations.
Information about the potential environmental and human health effects associated with substances present in the battery.
A URI referencing the technical manual detailing safe procedures for dismantling and removing the battery from its application.
A URI or reference document listing the manufacturer part numbers for all serviceable or replaceable battery components.
Details about where and how to obtain genuine spare parts or components for battery maintenance and repair.
Precautions and protective measures that must be observed when handling, storing, or working on the battery.
The percentage of nickel in the battery that originates from pre-consumer recycled sources such as manufacturing scrap.
The percentage of cobalt in the battery derived from pre-consumer recycled material streams.
The proportion of lithium content in the battery originating from pre-consumer recycling processes.
The percentage of nickel in the battery sourced from post-consumer recycled material collected after end-user use.
The proportion of cobalt in the battery that comes from post-consumer recycled material streams.
The share of lithium content in the battery derived from post-consumer recycled sources.
The percentage of lead used in the battery that has been recovered from recycled material sources.
The percentage of battery material content derived from renewable biological or sustainable resource streams.
A URI pointing to guidance for end-users on how to extend battery life and avoid unnecessary waste.
A URI linking to information for end-users about how and where to deposit spent batteries for separate collection.
A URI referencing comprehensive guidance on battery collection, preparation for second-life use, and proper end-of-life treatment.
The total electrical charge the battery can deliver under specified conditions as declared by the manufacturer, expressed in ampere-hours.
The charge capacity the battery can still deliver at the time of measurement, expressed in ampere-hours.
The percentage reduction in the battery's charge capacity relative to its original rated capacity.
The minimum usable energy the battery is certified to store and deliver, expressed in watt-hours.
The amount of usable energy the battery can still deliver at the point of measurement, expressed in watt-hours.
The ratio of remaining usable energy to the certified usable energy, expressed as a percentage.
The current charge level of the battery expressed as a percentage of its full charge capacity.
The lowest terminal voltage the battery may reach during discharge without causing damage or loss of function.
The highest terminal voltage permitted during charging without exceeding safe operating limits.
The representative voltage at which the battery is rated to operate under normal load conditions.
The maximum power output the battery was capable of delivering when new, expressed in watts.
The current maximum power output the battery is able to deliver at the point of measurement, in watts.
The reduction in maximum power capability since the battery left the factory, expressed in watts.
The upper limit of power the battery is permitted to deliver or absorb without risk of damage or unsafe operation.
The ratio of the battery's nominal power to its nominal energy, indicating how power-dense or energy-dense the design is.
The ratio of energy discharged to energy charged over a complete charge-discharge cycle when the battery was new, expressed as a percentage.
The measured round-trip energy efficiency at the midpoint of the battery's expected charge-discharge cycle life.
The current round-trip energy efficiency of the battery measured at the time of reporting.
The percentage decline in round-trip energy efficiency compared to the battery's initial efficiency when new.
The rate at which the battery loses charge spontaneously when idle, measured when the battery was new, expressed as a percentage per unit time.
The measured rate of spontaneous charge loss in the battery at the time of reporting.
A value or trend describing how the battery's self-discharge rate has changed over its operational lifetime.
The electrical resistance measured within the battery cell, module, or pack when first commissioned, expressed in ohms.
The percentage rise in battery internal resistance relative to its initial value, reflecting degradation over use.
The number of calendar years the battery is designed to remain in service under normal operating conditions.
The number of full charge-discharge cycles the battery is designed to complete before reaching end-of-life criteria.
The total number of complete charge-discharge cycles the battery has undergone since first use.
The name or designation of the standardised test procedure used to determine the battery's expected cycle life.
The charge or discharge C-rate applied during the cycle-life reference test, expressed in amperes per ampere-hour.
The cumulative amount of energy delivered by the battery over its entire operational life, expressed in watt-hours.
The total electrical charge the battery has delivered throughout its life, expressed in ampere-hours.
The minimum percentage of rated capacity below which the battery is considered exhausted or unsuitable for its intended application.
General temperature-related information relevant to the battery's safe and optimal operation.
The minimum ambient temperature at which the battery can be stored or operated without adverse effects.
The maximum ambient temperature at which the battery can be stored or operated without risk of damage.
The cumulative duration in seconds the battery has been exposed to temperatures above the defined upper boundary.
The total time in seconds the battery has been exposed to temperatures below the defined lower boundary.
The cumulative seconds the battery has been actively charging while the temperature exceeded the upper operating boundary.
The total seconds the battery spent charging while exposed to temperatures below the lower operating boundary.
The number of occurrences where the battery was discharged below the minimum safe voltage threshold.
The count of instances where the battery was charged beyond its maximum permitted voltage or state of charge.
A URI linking to documented information about any accidents or adverse incidents involving the battery.
